title: 8 PaymentStream芒聞垄 Direct Bill Payments Functions
body_text:
8	PaymentStream芒聞垄 Direct Bill Payments Functions
8.1	Overview of PaymentStream Direct Bill Payments Functions
To access the bill payment functions described in this Chapter, your banking system must be connected to
PaymentStream
Direct. Authorized
PaymentStream
Direct users can perform the following bill payment functions on behalf of a customer:
Add and delete payees
Create one-time bill payments
Schedule recurring (i.e., future-dated) bill payments
View and delete scheduled bill payments
Customers can see the transactions and tasks initiated by
PaymentStream
Direct users when they log in to their account via online banking. For example, if a
PaymentStream
Direct user adds a new bill payment payee, the customer can select that payee the next time they make an electronic bill payment.
8.2	Adding a Payee
PaymentStream
Direct users can add bill payment payees on behalf of a customer. To add a payee to the customer芒聙聶s account:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
Add a Payee for a Member
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select the Add a Payee for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
Add Payee
in the navigation menu. See Figure 2.
Note: 	The Add Payee link is also available on other Payments pages (e.g., the Pay Bills page).
Figure 2: Access Add Payee via the Payments Link
Enter all or part of the payee芒聙聶s name on the Search by Payee Name tab and select
Search
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Enter Payee Name
Alternatively, go to the Browse by Type tab and select a payee type. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Select Payee Type
Select the name of the payee that the customer wants to add. See Figure 5.
Note:	If searching by payee type, all payees in the selected category are listed in alphabetical order. Scroll to locate a payee or use the letter links at the top of the page to navigate.
Figure 5: Select Payee Name
Enter the customer芒聙聶s payee account number and select
Submit
. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Enter Account Number
The receipt is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Print Receipt
8.3	Deleting a Payee
PaymentStream
Direct users can delete bill payment payees on behalf of a customer. To delete a payee from the customer芒聙聶s account:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
Delete a Payee for a Member
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Select the Delete a Payee for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
Remove Payee
in the navigation menu. See Figure 9.
Note: 	The Delete Payee link is also available on other Payments pages (e.g., the Pay Bills page).
Figure 9: Access Remove Payee via Payments Link
Select
Delete
beside the appropriate payee. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Delete Payee
Select
Confirm
. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Confirm Payee Deletion
The receipt is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Print Receipt
8.4	Creating One-Time Bill Payments
PaymentStream
Direct users can create bill payments on behalf of a customer. To create a one-time bill payment:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
Pay Bills for a Member
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Select the Pay Bills for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
Pay Bills
in the navigation menu. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Access Pay Bills via the Payments Link
Complete the Pay Bills page as follows and then select
Pay Bills
. See Figure 15.
芒聢聮
Pay From
芒聙聯 Use the Pay From drop-down menu to select the account that the customer wants debited for the bill payment.
芒聢聮
Default account
芒聙聯
Select the box beside 芒聙聹Make this my default account to pay bills from芒聙聺 if the customer regularly pays bills from that account.
芒聢聮
Pay To
芒聙聯
Check the box beside the payee(s) that the customer wants to pay.
芒聢聮
Payment Date
芒聙聯
The payment date defaults to the current date. To create a one-time scheduled (i.e., future-dated) bill payment, use the calendar icon to change the payment date.
芒聢聮
Amount
芒聙聯
Enter the amounts of the bill payments.
Figure 15: Making a Bill Payment
Review the payment details and select
Confirm
to create the bill payment. See Figure 16.
Note:	Select
Edit
to change the payment details or
Cancel
to cancel
the payment.
Figure 16: Confirm Payment
The receipt is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Print Receipt
8.5	Creating Recurring Bill Payments
PaymentStream
Direct users can create recurring bill payments on behalf of a customer. These bill payments occur at regular intervals over a specified period of time.
To create a recurring bill payment:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
Schedule a Recurring Bill Payment for a Member
from the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Select the Schedule a Recurring Bill Payment for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
Schedule Recurring Bill
in the navigation menu. See Figure 19.
Note:	The Schedule Recurring Payment function is also available on the Pay Bills page.
Figure 19: Access Schedule Recurring Payments via the Payments Link
Complete the Schedule Recurring Payment page as follows and then select
Schedule
. See Figure 20.
芒聢聮
Pay From
芒聙聯 Use the Pay From drop-down menu to select the account that the customer wants debited for the bill payment.
芒聢聮
Default account
芒聙聯
Select the box beside 芒聙聹Make this my default account to pay bills from芒聙聺 if the customer regularly pays bills from that account.
芒聢聮
Pay To
芒聙聯
Use the Pay To drop-down menu to select the payee that the customer wants to pay.
芒聢聮
Amount
芒聙聯
Enter the amount of the bill payment.
芒聢聮
Start Date
芒聙聯
Use the calendar icon to select the start date.
芒聢聮
Payment Frequency
芒聙聯
Use the Payment Frequency drop-down menu to select the frequency of the payment (e.g., weekly, bi-weekly, monthly).
芒聢聮
End Date
芒聙聯
Use the calendar icon to select the payment end date.
Figure 20: Scheduling a Recurring Payment
Review the payment details and select
Confirm
to create the recurring payment. See Figure 21.
Note:	Select
Cancel
to cancel
the recurring payment.
Figure 21: Confirm Recurring Payment
The confirmation message is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 22.
Figure 22: Print Receipt
8.6	Viewing Recent and Scheduled Bill Payments
PaymentStream
Direct users can view a customer芒聙聶s recent bill payments and scheduled (i.e., future-dated) bill payments. Access to this information allows
PaymentStream
Direct users to answer customers芒聙聶 bill payment questions while working in
PaymentStream
Direct.
To view recent and scheduled bill payments:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
Payments
in the navigation menu and then select
Recent
or
Scheduled
, as appropriate on the Pay Bills page. See Figure 23.
Figure 23: Select Recent or Scheduled
Recent Bill Payments
芒聙聯 Selecting
Recent
brings the user to the Account Activity page. The search criteria fields are pre-populated and bill payments made within the default date range are displayed. See Figure 24.
Note: 	Users can also view recent bill payments by going directly to the Account Activity page as described in Section 7.4, Account Activity.
Figure 24: Recent Bill Payments
Scheduled Bill Payments
芒聙聯 Selecting
Scheduled
brings the user to the Scheduled Bill Payments page. See Figure 25.
Note: 	Users can also view scheduled bill payments by going directly to the Account Summary page as described in Section 7.3, Account Summary.
Figure 25: Scheduled Bill Payments
8.7	Deleting Scheduled Bill Payments
PaymentStream
Direct users can delete scheduled (i.e., future-dated) bill payments on behalf of a customer. To delete a scheduled bill payment:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
Select
Payments
in the navigation menu and then select
Scheduled
on the Pay Bills page. See Figure 26.
Figure 26: Select Scheduled
Select
Delete
beside the appropriate scheduled bill payment. See Figure 27.
Figure 27: Delete Scheduled Bill Payment
Review the confirmation page and select
Confirm
to delete the scheduled payment. See Figure 28.
Figure 28: Confirm Deletion
The receipt is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 29.
Figure 29: Print Receipt
8.8	Adding and Deleting CRA Accounts
PaymentStream
Direct CRA is connected to your banking system and allows users to add and delete Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) accounts for tax filing and payments on behalf of a customer for the following five forms:
GST/HST 34 Filing and Remittances
GST/HST Amount Owing Remittances (RC159)
GST/HST Interim Payments Remittances (RC160)
CRA Corporation Taxes (RC159/RC160)
Payroll and Source Deductions 芒聙聯 Current Year
Note:	These procedures are not related to Business Taxes, which is a different product on the
PaymentStream
Direct platform and isn芒聙聶t connected to banking systems. See Chapter 12, CRA Business Taxes, for more information.
Viewing a Customer芒聙聶s CRA Accounts
To view CRA accounts on the customer芒聙聶s behalf:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
From the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu, select
File and Pay CRA for a Member
. See Figure 30.
Figure 30: Select the File and Pay CRA for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
CRA Filing & Payments
from the navigation menu. See Figure 31.
Figure 31: Access CRA Filing & Payments via the Payments Link
Adding a CRA Tax Account
To add a CRA Tax Account:
Select
Add Account
beside the appropriate account type (e.g., GST/HST 34 Filing and Remittance). See Figure 32.
Figure 32: Add CRA Account
Enter the CRA account number in the Business Number field and select
Add Account
. See Figure 33.
Figure 33: Enter CRA Account Number
A confirmation message is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 34.
Figure 34: Print Receipt
Deleting a CRA Tax Account
To delete a CRA Tax Account:
Select
Delete Account
beside the appropriate account. See Figure 35.
Figure 35: Select Delete Account
Select
Delete
. See Figure 36.
Figure 36: Delete CRA Account
A confirmation message is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 37.
Figure 37: Print Receipt
8.9	Filing and Paying GST/HST 34 Taxes
The Process a Filing option allows a
PaymentStream
Direct user to initiate and complete a payment and/or a filing by entering all the required data at the time of the transaction. That is, the user completes the transaction while the customer waits. This is known as the GST/HST 34 long form. Authorization of these transactions on
PaymentStream
Direct isn芒聙聶t required.
To file and, optionally, pay GST/HST 34 taxes via the long form:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
From the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu, select
File and Pay CRA for a Member
. See Figure 38.
Figure 38: Select the File and Pay CRA for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
CRA Filing & Payments
from the navigation menu. See Figure 39.
Figure 39: Access CRA Filing & Payments via the Payments Link
Select
Process a Filing
in the GST/HST 34 Filing and Remittance section.
See Figure 40.
Figure 40: Process a Filing
Complete the File GST/HST 34 page and select
Continue
. Obtain the amounts for each field from CRA Form GST34-2, as provided by the customer. The field numbers displayed on
PaymentStream
Direct correspond to the field numbers on that form. See Figure 41.
Figure 41: Enter Data
Review the transaction details and select
Continue
to proceed.
See Figure 42.
Note:	Select
Edit
to change the transaction details or
Cancel
to cancel
the transaction.
Figure 42: Review GST/HST 34 Information
If the customer wants to make a payment, select the account to be debited and the payment date. The amount field is editable. Ensure that the filing option is File & Pay. This is the default selection.
If the customer doesn芒聙聶t want to make a payment, select
File Only
as the Filing Option. See Figure 43.
Select
Continue
.
Figure 43: Select Filing Option
Review the transaction details and select
Confirm
to proceed.
See Figure 44.
Figure 44: Confirm Transaction
A confirmation message is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 45.
Figure 45: Print Receipt
8.10	Paying GST/HST Amount Owing Remittance Vouchers (Form RC159)
The GST/HST Amount Owing Remittance function is used to make payments on an existing debt or to make a prepayment for an anticipated reassessment.
To pay GST/HST Amount Owning Remittances (Form RC159):
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
From the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu, select
File and Pay CRA for a Member
. See Figure 46.
Figure 46: Select the File and Pay CRA for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
CRA Filing & Payments
from the navigation menu. See Figure 47.
Figure 47: Access CRA Filing & Payments via the Payments Link
Select
Remit
in the GST/HST Amount Owing Remittance (RC 159) section. See Figure 48.
Figure 48: Remit
Complete the Remit RC 159 Amount Owing page and select
Continue
. Completion of this page is self-explanatory. See Figure 49.
Figure 49: Enter Details
Review the transaction details and select
Remit
to proceed.
See Figure 50.
Note:	Select
Edit
to change the transaction details or
Cancel
to cancel
the transaction.
Figure 50: Select Remit to Proceed
A confirmation message is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 51.
Figure 51: Print Receipt
8.11	Paying GST/HST Interim Payment Remittance Vouchers (Form RC160)
The GST/HST Interim Payment Remittances function is used to make interim payments for the tax year-end but only if a return hasn芒聙聶t been processed by the Canada Revenue Agency. Form RC160 shows the remittance period-end date (i.e., the customer芒聙聶s monthly installment payment due date, not the tax year-end date).
To pay GST/HST Interim Payment Remittances (Form RC 160):
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
From the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu, select
File and Pay CRA for a Member
. See Figure 52.
Figure 52: Select the File and Pay CRA for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
CRA Filing & Payments
from the navigation menu. See Figure 53.
Figure 53: Access CRA Filing & Payments via the Payments Link
Select
Remit
in the GST/HST Interim Payments Remittance (RC 160) section.
See Figure 54.
Figure 54: Remit
Complete the Remit RC 160 Interim Payments page and select
Continue
. Completion of this page is self-explanatory. See Figure 55.
Figure 55: Enter Details
Review the transaction details and select
Remit
to proceed.
See Figure 56.
Note:	Select
Edit
to change the transaction details or
Cancel
to cancel
the transaction.
Figure 56: Select Remit to Proceed
A confirmation message is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 57.
Figure 57: Print Receipt
8.12	Paying CRA Corporation Taxes
To pay the CRA Corporation Tax:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
From the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu, select
File and Pay CRA for a Member
. See Figure 58.
Figure 58: Select the File and Pay CRA for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
CRA Filing & Payments
from the navigation menu. See Figure 59.
Figure 59: Access CRA Filing & Payments via the Payments Link
Select
Remit
in the Corporation Tax (RC 159/RC 160) section.
See Figure 60.
Figure 60: Remit
Complete the Remit Corporation Tax page and select
Continue
. Completion of this page is self-explanatory. However, ensure that you select
Instalment
,
Arrears/Amount Owing
, or
Payment on Filing
from the Payment Type drop-down menu. See Figure 61.
Figure 61: Enter Details
Review the transaction details and select
Remit
to proceed.
See Figure 62.
Note:	Select
Edit
to change the transaction details or
Cancel
to cancel the transaction.
Figure 62: Select Remit to Proceed
A confirmation message is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 63.
Figure 63: Print Receipt
8.13	Paying CRA Payroll and Source Deductions
To pay CRA payroll and source deductions:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
From the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu, select
File and Pay CRA for a Member
. See Figure 64.
Figure 64: Select the File and Pay CRA for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
CRA Filing & Payments
from the navigation menu. See Figure 65.
Figure 65: Access CRA Filing & Payments via the Payments Link
Select
Remit
in the Payroll and Source Deduction 芒聙聯 Current Year section.
See Figure 66.
Figure 66: Remit
Complete the Remit Payroll and Source Deduction page and select
Continue
. See Figure 67. Completion of this page is self-explanatory, but note the following:
芒聢聮
Remittance Type
芒聙聯
Select
Regular
,
Accelerated 芒聙聯 Threshold 1
, or
Accelerated 芒聙聯 Threshold 2
.
芒聢聮
End of Remitting Period
芒聙聯
Select the Remitting Period end date and ensure that it is valid. See the table below for valid dates.
Remittance Type
Valid End Date for Remitting Period
Regular
Last day of the month
Accelerated 芒聙聯 Threshold 1
15th or last day of the month
Accelerated 芒聙聯 Threshold 2
7th, 14th, 21st, or last day of the month
Figure 67: Enter Details
Review the transaction details and select
Remit
to proceed.
See Figure 68.
Note:	Select
Edit
to change the transaction details or
Cancel
to cancel
the transaction.
Figure 68: Select Remit to Proceed
A confirmation message is displayed. To print a copy of the receipt for the customer, select
Print Receipt
. See Figure 69.
Figure 69: Print Receipt
8.14	Scheduled CRA Payments
As with other bill payments, customers can request scheduled (i.e., future-dated) CRA payments. GST/HST 34 and non-GST/HST 34 scheduled payments are handled differently:
If a customer cancels a scheduled GST/HST 34 payment or if there are insufficient funds in the source account on the due date, the filing information will still be sent to CRA even though the payment wasn芒聙聶t made.
If a scheduled non-GST/HST 34 payment is cancelled or if there are insufficient funds in the source account on the due date, no information is sent to CRA and the payment isn芒聙聶t made.
8.15	Viewing CRA Filings and Payments History
PaymentStream
Direct users can view a history of CRA filings and tax payments.
To view CRA filings and payments:
Access the customer芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 7.2, Accessing a Customer芒聙聶s Profile.
From the 芒聙聹Select a Task芒聙聺 drop-down menu, select
File and Pay CRA for a Member
. See Figure 70.
Figure 70: Select the File and Pay CRA for a Member Option
Alternatively, select
Payments
>
CRA Filing & Payments
from the navigation menu. See Figure 71.
Figure 71: Access CRA Filing & Payments via the Payments Link
Select
View History
beside the appropriate account number. See Figure 72.
Figure 72: View History
Select a date range and select
View History
. Data is available for 18 months. See Figure 73.
Figure 73: Select Date Range
View the search results and advise the customer of the results. See Figure 74.
Note:	The description on the CRA Account History page varies depending on the type of CRA account selected.
Figure 74: Search Results
8.16	Settlement and Reporting of Bill Payments
Bill payments made on
PaymentStream
Direct are settled and reported the same way as bill payments made through other electronic channels (e.g., online banking, ATMs, IVR). See the Electronic Bill Payments System Part for information on settlement and reports.
Use the Online Tracing System (OLT) to trace bill payments. See the Online Tracing System Part for more information.
END
